papers.title_en: TRIDENT: Breaking the Hybrid-Safety-Physics Coupling for Provably Safe Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
papers.authors: Zijie Meng, Ziwei Li, Yufei Liu, Zhiyu Li, Jiyuan Liu, Wenhua Nie, Bingcai Wei, Miao Zhang
papers.evidence: 使用Lyapunov约束实现可证明安全的多智能体RL，与控制屏障函数概念紧密相关
papers.tldr: 多智能体强化学习面临混合离散-连续动作、安全约束和物理动力学耦合的三重偏差循环。TRIDENT通过理查森-龙贝格梯度校正降低Gumbel-Softmax偏差、李雅普诺夫约束序贯信赖域更新保证每步可行、物理信息残差评论家分解价值，实现安全协调。实验显示训练违反减少95.5%，奖励提升13.5%。
papers.abstract_en: Safe coordination in networked cyber-physical systems forces learning algorithms to simultaneously handle hybrid discrete-continuous actions, hard training-time safety constraints, and physics-governed dynamics. We show that these three features form a directed cycle of biases that defeats any naive composition of off-the-shelf modules, and formalize this as a three-way coupling lemma. We then introduce TRIDENT, the first MARL framework whose three components are co-designed to cancel each leak: a Richardson-Romberg gradient correction reducing Gumbel-Softmax bias from O(tau) to O(tau^2), a Lyapunov-constrained sequential trust-region update enforcing per-iterate feasibility, and a physics-informed residual critic that decomposes value rather than reward. We prove an O~(1/sqrt(K)) convergence rate to a constrained Nash equilibrium and an O(sqrt(K)) cumulative-violation bound. On multi-UAV mobile-edge computing, autonomous intersection management, and a hybrid SMAC variant, TRIDENT cuts training-time violations by 95.5% over MADDPG and 76.3% over MACPO, while improving reward by 13.5% over the strongest unconstrained baseline.

papers.title_en: Distributed Safe Consensus Under Asymmetric Input and Time-Varying Output Constraints
papers.evidence: 使用障碍坐标变换的分布式安全共识
papers.tldr: 针对具有非对称执行器约束和时变输出安全约束的单积分器多智能体系统，提出一种分布式安全一致性控制方法。通过引入障碍坐标变换将时变安全区间映射到实数域，设计基于图论的协调层与执行器跟踪层相结合的控制律。理论证明在紧致初始集下闭环解完整，执行器输入严格在非对称界内，输出始终在安全区间内，且同步误差指数收敛。该方法首次同时处理非对称执行器饱和与时变输出约束，实现安全一致性。
papers.abstract_en: This paper studies safe distributed consensus for single-integrator multi-agent systems over connected undirected graphs under simultaneous asymmetric actuator constraints and output safety constraints. Each agent is equipped with a continuously differentiable asymmetric actuator dynamics that maps a commanded control signal to the realized plant input while keeping the latter strictly inside a prescribed admissible interval. To address output safety, a barrier-coordinate transformation is introduced over a common time-varying safe interval, and a distributed synchronization law is designed in the transformed coordinates. The resulting controller integrates a graph-based coordination layer with an actuator-side tracking layer, thereby enabling simultaneous enforcement of input admissibility, forward invariance of the safe output set, and asymptotic synchronization. For compact admissible sets of initial conditions, it is shown that the closed-loop solution is complete, all signals remain bounded, the actuator inputs remain strictly within their asymmetric bounds, and the agent outputs remain inside the prescribed safe interval for all time. Moreover, the transformed synchronization errors converge exponentially to zero, and the original agent outputs asymptotically synchronize to a designer-selected admissible trajectory embedded in the common safe interval. Numerical simulations validate the proposed framework and demonstrate safe consensus under both asymmetric actuation bounds and time-varying output constraints.

papers.title_en: Environment-Aware Stable Neural Koopman Dynamics Learning for Input-Driven Systems under Environmental Constraints
papers.evidence: 具有稳定性与安全相关保证的神经Koopman动力学学习
papers.tldr: 针对非线性动力系统建模需同时处理环境变化、稳定性保证与ISS认证的问题，本文提出环境感知稳定神经Koopman动力学（ESNKD）。该方法集成束结构编码器、输入条件神经ODE、收缩合成层和LMI基础ISS验证。在五个基准系统（含两个机器人平台）上，预测精度和安全认证率均优于五个强基线。首次在统一可训练框架内实现环境自适应、严格稳定性与ISS认证。
papers.abstract_en: Constructing predictive models of nonlinear dynamical systems from measurement data is a longstanding problem in systems identification and control. Although Neural ordinary differential equations~(Neural ODEs), Koopman operator approximations, and input-aware architectures have each moved the field forward, none simultaneously addresses environment-varying operating conditions, rigorous stability guarantees, and input-to-state stability (ISS) certification within a unified trainable framework. This paper introduces Environment-Aware Stable Neural Koopman Dynamics Learning (ESNKD), which integrates four components: (i)~a bundle-structured encoder that maps environmental observations to a geometrically regularized latent manifold, drawing on the fiber bundle framework; (ii)~an input-conditioned Neural ODE whose residual term handles arbitrary external signals, extending the input concomitant philosophy; (iii)~a contraction synthesis layer enforcing convergence via Persidskii-type tractable linear inequalities, analogous to the certification mechanism; and (iv)~a Koopman lifting stage with LMI-based ISS verification that follows the theoretical pipeline of. Theoretical guarantees cover solution existence and uniqueness, incremental exponential stability, ISS with explicit gain bounds, and robustness to environmental perturbation. Experiments on five benchmark systems, including two robotic manipulation platforms, show consistent improvements over five competitive baselines in both prediction accuracy and safety certification rates.
